drugs.notes: Twice daily (1600 mg/m²/day total). Days 1-14 inclusive. Start on evening of day 1. Take with or after food.
Loading dose cycle 1 day 1 only. Minimum 120 minutes at max rate 5 mg/min. Leave 60 minutes between cetuximab and oxaliplatin administration. Dose banded per national dose bands (5 mg/ml).

Maintenance dose 250 mg/m² days 8 and 15 cycle 1; days 1, 8, 15 from cycle 2 onwards. Minimum 60 minutes if well tolerated. Dose reductions to 200 mg/m² then 150 mg/m² for grade 3 skin toxicity (reductions are permanent). Dose banded (5 mg/ml).
IV infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. Must never contact sodium chloride 0.9%. Flush line with glucose 5% before and after. Dose banded (5 mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 30 ml/min
Hepatic impairment: transaminases more than 5xULN or bilirubin more than 1.5xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Administer only when transaminases 5xULN or below and bilirubin 1.5xULN or below

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 20 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Dose reduce and adjust according to toxicity

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophil less than 1.5x10^9/L or platelet less than 75x10^9/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay up to 7 days (restart full dose) or up to 14 days (restart 75%). Stop if delay exceeds 20 days.
Resume at 50% after 3rd. Stop if 4th occurrence.
Stop infusion; administer supportive therapy; resume at 50% of previous rate once resolved to grade 1 or below

dose_modifications.condition: Cetuximab NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 infusion reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Stop infusion immediately; do not re-administer cetuximab

dose_modifications.condition: Cetuximab NCI-CTC grade 3 acneiform skin rash (1st-2nd occurrence within 14 days)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay up to 14 days; resume at same dose if recovered. Dose reduce to 200 mg/m² (2nd occurrence) then 150 mg/m² (3rd). Reductions permanent. Discontinue if 4th episode grade 3 or any grade 4.

dose_modifications.condition: Oxaliplatin: cold-related dysaesthesia or paresthesia without pain persisting between cycles
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until recovery, restart at 100 mg/m². Omit if recurs.

dose_modifications.condition: Oxaliplatin: paresthesia with pain or functional impairment persisting beyond 7 days or NCI-CTC grade 3+
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 100 mg/m² in first instance. Omit if painful paresthesia recurs or persists between cycles.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV (30 minutes prior to cetuximab; counts as antiemetic on day 1)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
H2 antagonist per local formulary choice (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (after cetuximab, prior to oxaliplatin, on day 1)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home, starting day after oxaliplatin)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home)
special_warnings: This regimen may require funding approval
Wild-type KRAS genotype must be confirmed positive before starting treatment
DPD testing required: all patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1) — complete or partial deficiency increases risk of severe and fatal toxicity with capecitabine
Cetuximab dose reductions are permanent once made
Cetuximab: discontinue if more than two consecutive infusions withheld, or a 4th grade 3 skin toxicity episode, or any grade 4 toxicity at any time
Monitor for hypersensitivity for 60 minutes after cetuximab infusion; do not administer other chemotherapy during this period
UV radiation may worsen cetuximab skin reactions; sun safety practices required during and up to 2 months after treatment end
Stop treatment if confirmed pneumonitis develops
Oxaliplatin must never contact sodium chloride 0.9%; use glucose 5% only
Rare risk of acute interstitial lung disease with oxaliplatin; stop treatment if unexplained respiratory symptom and investigate
Consider stopping capecitabine if chest pain occurs
Capecitabine: total daily dose divided into two doses 12 hours apart; serious toxicity has occurred when total daily dose given incorrectly as twice-daily
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy prescribing, dispensing and administration

drugs.notes: Twice daily (1250 mg/m²/day total). Days 1-21 inclusive. Start on evening of day 1. Take with or after food.
IV infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride at maximum rate 1 mg cisplatin per minute (minimum 120 minutes). Pre and post hydration required. Dose banded (1 mg/ml).
Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Vesicant. Dose banded (2 mg/ml PM).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 30 ml/min
Renal impairment CrCl less than 45 ml/min
Hepatic impairment: bilirubin more than 51 umol/L

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce doses in cases of severe renal impairment

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 neutropenia and/or NCI-CTC grade 2 thrombocytopenia
dose_modifications.notes: Stop all treatment; restart capecitabine and cisplatin at last dose; reduce epirubicin by 25%

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 4 neutropenia or NCI-CTC grade 3 thrombocytopenia
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery; restart capecitabine and cisplatin at last dose; reduce epirubicin to 50%
Stop epirubicin permanently. Restart capecitabine and cisplatin at last dose.
Delay all chemotherapy until counts recover; restart at 75% of previous epirubicin dose
Delay all chemotherapy until counts recover; restart at 50% of previous epirubicin dose

dose_modifications.condition: Cisplatin neurotoxicity NCI-CTC grade 2+ or new functional hearing/tinnitus deterioration

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Furosemide 40mg oral or IV (pre-cisplatin hydration)
1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 60 minutes (pre-hydration)
1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 60 minutes (post-hydration)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home, starting day 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home)
special_warnings: DPD testing required: all patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1) — complete or partial deficiency increases risk of severe and fatal toxicity with capecitabine
Epirubicin cumulative lifetime dose limit approximately 900 mg/m² — monitor cumulative dose to avoid cardiotoxicity
Baseline LVEF must be measured before starting therapy, particularly in patients with history of cardiac problems or the elderly
Capecitabine: total daily dose divided into two doses 12 hours apart; first dose in evening of day 1
Cisplatin: patients must drink at least 3 litres of fluid in the 24 hours after administration
Cisplatin neurotoxicity grade 2+ or new hearing/tinnitus impairment: substitute with carboplatin AUC5
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy

drugs.notes: Twice daily (1250 mg/m²/day total). Days 1-21 inclusive. Start on evening of day 1. Take with or after food.

IV infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride at maximum rate 1 mg cisplatin per minute (minimum 120 minutes). Pre and post hydration required. Radiotherapy given during cycles 3 and 4 only (normally commencing day 1 of cycle 3, Monday). Cisplatin cycles 3 and 4 administered day 1 or 2 (Monday or Tuesday). Dose banded (1 mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 30 ml/min
Renal impairment CrCl less than 45 ml/min
Haematological: subsequent cycles — 14-day delay required OR two separate 7-day delays
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce cisplatin to 80% of original dose

dose_modifications.condition: Cisplatin neurotoxicity NCI-CTC grade 2+ or new hearing/tinnitus impairment

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Furosemide 40mg oral or IV (pre-cisplatin hydration)
1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 60 minutes (pre-hydration)
1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 60 minutes (post-hydration)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home, starting day 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home)
special_warnings: IMPORTANT: A separate Capecitabine-Cisplatin regimen exists without radiotherapy for upper GI cancers — confirm correct regimen before prescribing
DPD testing required: all patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1) — complete or partial deficiency increases risk of severe and fatal toxicity with capecitabine
Radiotherapy given during cycles 3 and 4 only; radiotherapy normally commences day 1 of cycle 3 (Monday)
Day 1 of treatment should where possible be scheduled on Monday or Tuesday
Capecitabine: total daily dose divided into two doses 12 hours apart; first dose in evening of day 1
Cisplatin: patients must drink at least 3 litres of fluid in the 24 hours after administration
Cisplatin neurotoxicity grade 2+ or new hearing/tinnitus: substitute with carboplatin
Consider formal audiology test if relevant
Consider formally measuring GFR prior to treatment with cisplatin on day 1 of cycle 1
Blood transfusion: consider if haemoglobin less than 12g/dL during radiotherapy
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy

drugs.notes: Twice daily (2000 mg/m²/day total). Days 1-14 inclusive. Start on evening of day 1. Take with or after food. Given for maximum 6 cycles.

IV infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride at maximum rate 1 mg cisplatin per minute (minimum 120 minutes). Pre and post hydration required. Given for maximum 6 cycles. Dose banded (1 mg/ml).

Cycle 1 loading dose 8 mg/kg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 90 minutes. Cycle 2 onwards maintenance dose 6 mg/kg over 30 minutes (or 90 minutes if not well tolerated). Trastuzumab continues until disease progression; after 6 cycles switch to single-agent trastuzumab regimen. Observe patient for 6 hours after start of first infusion, 2 hours after subsequent infusions. Dose banded (21 mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 30 ml/min
Renal impairment CrCl less than 45 ml/min
Trastuzumab: no dose adjustment for renal or hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: No dose reduction necessary for hepatic or renal impairment

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: 14-day delay required OR two separate 7-day delays
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce cisplatin to 80% of original dose

dose_modifications.condition: Cisplatin neurotoxicity NCI-CTC grade 2+ or new hearing/tinnitus impairment
LVEF drop confirmed: LVEF drop ≥10 points AND LVEF less than 50%
dose_modifications.notes: Stop trastuzumab. Symptomatic cardiac dysfunction: discontinue trastuzumab, start ACE inhibitor therapy, refer to cardiologist.

pre_medications: Trastuzumab administered first (see notes)
Aprepitant 125mg oral (15-30 minutes prior to cisplatin chemotherapy, day 1)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to cisplatin chemotherapy, day 1)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to cisplatin chemotherapy, day 1)
Furosemide 40mg oral or IV (pre-cisplatin hydration)
1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 60 minutes (pre-hydration)
1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 60 minutes (post-hydration)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV when required for trastuzumab infusion reactions (once only)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for trastuzumab infusion reactions (once only)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral when required for trastuzumab infusion reactions (once only)
Aprepitant 80mg once daily oral for 2 days (take home, starting day 2)
Dexamethasone 4mg once daily oral for 3 days (take home, starting day 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home)
special_warnings: Confirm positive HER2 status prior to starting therapy — treatment only indicated for HER2-positive metastatic adenocarcinoma of stomach or gastro-oesophageal junction
DPD testing required: all patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1) — complete or partial deficiency increases risk of severe and fatal toxicity with capecitabine

Trastuzumab cardiotoxicity monitoring: LVEF must be 50% or above before starting cycle 1. Assess cardiac function prior to starting trastuzumab and 12-weekly thereafter (or 4-8 weekly if signs of cardiac impairment). Use LVEF flowchart for management of asymptomatic LVEF decline.
Trastuzumab dose reductions not recommended — dose modification is by delay or discontinuation only
Trastuzumab continues until disease progression; after 6 cycles of combination, switch to single-agent trastuzumab regimen
Capecitabine and cisplatin given for maximum 6 cycles
If trastuzumab missed by 14 days or less: give maintenance dose (6 mg/kg) as soon as possible without waiting for next planned cycle
If trastuzumab missed by more than 14 days: re-loading dose 8 mg/kg over 90 minutes then resume schedule
Trastuzumab hypersensitivity: observe for 6 hours after start of first infusion; 2 hours after subsequent infusions
Capecitabine: total daily dose divided into two doses 12 hours apart; first dose in evening of day 1. Consider stopping capecitabine if chest pain occurs.
Cisplatin: patients must drink at least 3 litres of fluid in the 24 hours after administration
Cisplatin neurotoxicity grade 2+ or new hearing/tinnitus impairment: substitute with carboplatin
Patients should be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting treatment
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy

drugs.notes: Twice daily (2000 mg/m²/day total). Days 1-14 inclusive. Start on evening of day 1. Take with or after food. Dose banded per national dose bands.
IV infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride at maximum rate 1 mg cisplatin per minute (minimum 120 minutes). Pre and post hydration required. Dose banded (1 mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl 30 ml/min or less
Renal impairment CrCl less than 45 ml/min
Hepatic impairment: bilirubin more than 3xULN or AST/ALT more than 2.5xULN (treatment-related)
dose_modifications.notes: Consider delaying treatment if hepatic dysfunction is treatment-related. For mild/moderate dysfunction due to liver metastasis, dose modification may not be necessary.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: 14-day delay required OR two separate 7-day delays
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce cisplatin to 80% of original dose

dose_modifications.condition: Cisplatin neurotoxicity NCI-CTC grade 2+ or new hearing/tinnitus impairment

Dexamethasone 4mg once daily oral for 3 days (take home, starting day 2)

Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home, starting evening day 1)
special_warnings: DPD testing required: all patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1) — complete or partial deficiency increases risk of severe and fatal toxicity with capecitabine
Capecitabine: total daily dose divided into two doses 12 hours apart; first dose in evening of day 1

NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy

drugs.notes: Twice daily (1250 mg/m²/day total). Days 1-21 inclusive. Start on evening of day 1. Take with or after food.
Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Vesicant. Dose banded (2 mg/ml PM).
IV infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. Glucose 5% flush before and after oxaliplatin administration. Dose banded (5 mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 30 ml/min

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce doses in cases of severe renal impairment
Consider dose adjustments according to toxicity in moderate renal impairment

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: bilirubin more than 51 umol/L
Hepatic impairment: bilirubin more than 51 umol/L
Haematological: NCI-CTC grade 1 thrombocytopenia and/or grade 1-2 neutropenia — 7-day delay
dose_modifications.notes: Reinitiate at full epirubicin dose for 7-day delay; reduce oxaliplatin to 100 mg/m² for 14-day delay

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: NCI-CTC grade 2+ thrombocytopenia or grade 3+ neutropenia
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce epirubicin to 75% and reduce oxaliplatin to 100 mg/m²
Delay until eligible; resume at 50% epirubicin and oxaliplatin 100 mg/m²
Stop epirubicin. Resume with oxaliplatin 100 mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: Oxaliplatin: cold-related dysaesthesia/paresthesia without pain persisting between cycles
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until recovery, restart at 100 mg/m². Omit if recurs.

dose_modifications.condition: Oxaliplatin: paresthesia with pain or functional impairment persisting beyond 7 days or NCI-CTC grade 3+
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 100 mg/m² in first instance. Omit if painful paresthesia recurs or persists. Carboplatin AUC5 may be substituted — if so, stop epirubicin to avoid myelosuppression.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3-4 diarrhoea or stomatitis recurring despite capecitabine dose reduction

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, day 1)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, day 1)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home, starting day 2)

Epirubicin cumulative lifetime dose limit approximately 900 mg/m² — monitor cumulative dose to avoid cardiotoxicity
Baseline LVEF must be measured before starting therapy, particularly in patients with history of cardiac problems or the elderly
Capecitabine: total daily dose divided into two doses 12 hours apart; first dose in evening of day 1
If carboplatin substituted for oxaliplatin, epirubicin must be stopped to avoid undue myelosuppression
Oxaliplatin: rare risk of acute interstitial lung disease or lung fibrosis; stop treatment if unexplained respiratory symptoms and investigate
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy

drugs.notes: Twice daily (1660 mg/m²/day total). Days 1-21 inclusive (28-day cycle). Start on evening of day 1. Take with or after food. Dose banded per national dose bands.
IV infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes on days 1, 8, and 15. Dose banded (100 mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 30 ml/min
Renal impairment CrCl less than 30 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose adjustments when CrCl is less than 30 ml/min

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: bilirubin raised (treatment-related or significant elevation)
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reductions especially where bilirubin is raised

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological on day of gemcitabine: neutrophils 0.5-1x10^9/L and/or platelets 50-100x10^9/L
dose_modifications.notes: Administer 75% of original gemcitabine dose on that day. Dose may be re-escalated to 100% on next administration if blood count adequate.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological on day of gemcitabine: neutrophils less than 0.5x10^9/L and/or platelets less than 50x10^9/L
dose_modifications.notes: Omit gemcitabine for 7 days. Do not replace omitted dose; maintain cycle schedule.
Withhold until fully recovered; reinstate at 75% with no further re-escalation
Further reduction to 50% of full dose

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to gemcitabine on days 1, 8, 15)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home, day 1 only)
special_warnings: Disease site: Pancreatic Cancer — adjuvant treatment of pancreatic cancer and first-line treatment of advanced pancreatic cancer
DPD testing required: all patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1) — complete or partial deficiency increases risk of severe and fatal toxicity with capecitabine
Capecitabine: total daily dose divided into two doses 12 hours apart; first dose in evening of day 1
Gemcitabine dose escalation back to 100% is permitted if blood count is adequate on day of next administration (after a 75% reduction for low counts — not after neutropenic sepsis reduction)
Where gemcitabine dose omissions occur the dose should not be replaced; patients maintain same cycle schedule
Consider stopping capecitabine if chest pain occurs
FBC, U&Es and LFTs required prior to each treatment on days 1, 8, 15
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy

drugs.notes: Twice daily (2000 mg/m²/day total). Days 1-14 inclusive. Start on evening of day 1. Take with or after food. Dose banded per national dose bands.

IV infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 90 minutes (can be administered over 30-90 minutes). Maximum dose 700 mg. Consider attenuated starting doses for patients aged 70+, PS 2, or with prior radical pelvic radiotherapy. Dose banded (20 mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 30 ml/min
Hepatic impairment: bilirubin more than 51 umol/L
Hepatic impairment: bilirubin less than 26 umol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Full dose 250 mg/m² when bilirubin less than 26 umol/L

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophils less than 1.5x10^9/L and/or platelets less than 100x10^9/L — 7-day delay; counts recover
dose_modifications.notes: Restart capecitabine at original dose; reduce irinotecan to 80%

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: 14-day delay required OR two separate 7-day delays
dose_modifications.notes: Consider reducing irinotecan to 50% or stopping treatment

dose_modifications.condition: Asymptomatic nadir neutrophil count less than 0.5x10^9/L or febrile neutropenia episode
NCI-CTC grade 3 or above irinotecan toxicity (any toxicity — next cycle)
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce irinotecan to 80%. Next cycle must not start until all toxicities resolved to NCI-CTC grade 0-1. Diarrhoea must have fully resolved.

pre_medications: Atropine 250mcg subcutaneous (immediately prior to irinotecan — prevention of acute cholinergic syndrome)
Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, day 1 only)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy, day 1 only)
Atropine 250mcg subcutaneous when required (relief of cholinergic symptoms)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home, starting day after irinotecan)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home)
special_warnings: DPD testing required: all patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1) — complete or partial deficiency increases risk of severe and fatal toxicity with capecitabine
Atropine 250mcg SC must be given immediately prior to irinotecan to prevent acute cholinergic syndrome; a further 250mcg may be given to relieve cholinergic symptoms if they develop

Irinotecan delayed diarrhoea: administer oral loperamide 2mg every 2 hours from first liquid stool until 12 hours after last liquid stool (maximum 48 hours use). Consider oral ciprofloxacin 500mg twice daily if diarrhoea continues more than 24 hours.
Maximum dose of irinotecan is 700 mg
Consider attenuated starting doses for patients aged 70 or over, PS 2, or who have had prior radical radiotherapy to the pelvis
Diarrhoea must have fully resolved to NCI-CTC grade 0-1 before next treatment cycle can begin
Capecitabine: total daily dose divided into two doses 12 hours apart; first dose in evening of day 1. Consider stopping capecitabine if chest pain occurs.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy
